NOTICE OF HEARING AND AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PETITION FOR WRIT OF PROHIBITION FROM THE SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S CALIFORNIA DRIVER'S LICENSE FILED BY PETITIONER CARTER JR., BOBBY JOE HEARING SET FOR JUN-17-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY PETITIONER CARTER JR., BOBBY JOE

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER BOBBY CARTER'S AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PETITION FOR WRIT OF PROHIBITION FROM THE SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S CALIFORNIA DRIVER'S LICENSE IS DENIED. THE FINDINGS OF THE HEARING OFFICER ARE SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE TO WARRANT SUSPENSION PER VEHICLE CODE 13557(B)(3). THE ARRESTING OFFICERS HAD REASONABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE MR. CARTER BEEN DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF ALCOHOL BECAUSE HE ADMITTED TO THE POLICE OFFICERS THAT HE HAD BEEN DRIVING AND THE POLICE OFFICERS SMELLED THE STRONG ODOR OF ALCOHOL ON HIS BREATH. (INCIDENT REPORT, P. 6; DS 367, P. 1.) MR. CARTER WAS LAWFULLY ARRESTED BASED ON HIS ADMISSION AND THE OFFICER'S OBSERVATIONS. (VEH. CODE 40300.5.) MR. CARTER HAD A BAC OF MORE THAN 0.08; THE RESULTS OF HIS BREATH TESTS WERE .148 BAC AT 2118 HOURS AND 145 BAC AT 2120. (INCIDENT REPORT, P. 6, DS 367, P. 1.) MR. CARTER HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN TO REBUT THE PRESUMPTION THAT AN OFFICIAL DUTY HAS BEEN REGULARLY PERFORMED. (EVID. CODE 664.) ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT WHICH INCLUDES COST. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-17-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate, Or In The Alternative, Petition For Writ Of Prohibition From The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License
Off Calendar Jun-17-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. E. MICHAEL LINSCHEID, TEL. NO. (510) 451-2010 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. COURTNEY LUI, TEL. NO. (510) 622-2115 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER BOBBY CARTER'S Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate, Or In The Alternative, Petition For Writ Of Prohibition From The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License is Denied. The findings of the hearing officer are supported by the weight of the evidence to warrant suspension per Vehicle Code 13557(b)(3). The arresting officers had reasonable cause to believe Mr. Carter been driving under the influence of alcohol because he admitted to the police officers that he had been driving and the police officers smelled the strong odor of alcohol on his breath. (Incident Report, p. 6; DS 367, p. 1.) Mr. Carter was lawfully arrested based on his admission and the officer's observations. (Veh. Code 40300.5.) Mr. Carter had a BAC of more than 0.08; the results of his breath tests were .148 BAC at 2118 hours and 145 BAC at 2120. (Incident Report, p. 6, DS 367, p. 1.) Mr. Carter has not met his burden to rebut the presumption that an official duty has been regularly performed. (Evid. Code 664.)
ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT WHICH INCLUDES COST. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
